name: textgrad
prompt:
Here is the role of the variable you will improve: <ROLE>Retrieval pipeline configuration for the 3-layer GIST/Fact/Premise/Syllogism hybrid search system. Contains a JSON config block and rationale.</ROLE>.

The variable is the text within the following span: <VARIABLE> You are configuring the 3-layer GIST retrieval pipeline to maximize (...) gives 169-document candidate pools (Fibonacci cascade: 13²=169, φ×169≈104 after GIST)."
}
```
 </VARIABLE>

Here is the context and feedback we got for the variable:

<CONTEXT>Evaluation result:
  Composite score: 0.0000/1.0 (loss=1.0000)

Metric breakdown:
  - faithfulness: 0.0000 (gap to 1.0: 1.0000)
  - context_precision: 0.0000 (gap to 1.0: 1.0000)
  - context_recall: 0.0000 (gap to 1.0: 1.0000)
  - answer_relevancy: 0.0000 (gap to 1.0: 1.0000)

Current pipeline settings: rrf_k=60, gist_lambda=0.70, layer2=OFF, colbert=ON, cross_encoder=ON, top_k=13

The weakest metrics are: faithfulness and context_precision.
To close the gap, consider adjusting these parameters:
  - If faithfulness is low: try increasing top_k or lowering bm25_min_score
    so more relevant chunks are included.
  - If context_precision is low: try raising bm25_min_score or lowering rrf_k
    (≤40) to make top-rank results dominate the fusion.
  - If context_recall is low: enable use_layer2_expansion=true to bring in
    triplet-expanded chunks, or increase top_k.
  - If answer_relevancy is low: try use_colbert=true and/or
    use_cross_encoder=true for tighter reranking.
  - gist_lambda: increase toward 1.0 for more relevance, decrease for more diversity.

Update the JSON configuration block to improve, and provide an updated rationale.</CONTEXT>

Improve the variable (Retrieval pipeline configuration for the 3-layer GIST/Fact/Premise/Syllogism hybrid search system. Contains a JSON config block and rationale.) using the feedback provided in <FEEDBACK> tags.
Send the improved variable in the following format:

<IMPROVED_VARIABLE>{the improved variable}</IMPROVED_VARIABLE>

Send ONLY the improved variable between the <IMPROVED_VARIABLE> tags, and nothing else.
